title: scholar-simulate run manifest
description: Single source of truth for one simulation run. All fields that affect outputs are pinned here for reproducibility.

run_id.description: Unique run identifier; also the default checkpoint subdirectory name.

paradigm.description: Which simulation paradigm this run implements. 'interactive' = small-N multi-agent conversation served by assets/interactive_runner.py (LangGraph), NOT the batch engine.

model.description: Exact, pinned model id (e.g., claude-haiku-4-5, gpt-4o-mini, llama3.1).

max_tokens.description: Output token cap per call (cost control).

scale_strategy.description: batch=managed Batch API (anthropic/openai, ~50% off); async=concurrent cloud calls; local=async vs a local server.

cache.description: Prompt-cache the system+persona prefix so a persona's repeated items/reps do not re-pay input tokens.

conditions.description: Experimental arms. Omit for a single base condition.

n_reps.description: Responses per (persona,item) to capture within-persona stochasticity.

checkpoint_dir.description: Where responses.jsonl / cost-ledger.jsonl / batch-state.json are written.

cost_cap_usd.description: Hard ceiling: the engine refuses to run if the estimate exceeds this (ignored for local).

price_per_mtok.description: Optional [input,output] USD per 1M tokens override for cost estimation.

flush_every.description: Async path: responses per checkpoint flush.

agents.description: INTERACTIVE paradigm only: the conversational roles. Each is a node in the LangGraph multi-agent graph.

id.description: Unique agent id; appears in the transcript custom_id.

persona_ref.description: Optional persona_id from personas.jsonl to ground this agent.

system.description: System prompt / role brief for this agent.

topology.description: INTERACTIVE paradigm only: the LangGraph graph shape governing turn order.

max_turns.description: INTERACTIVE paradigm only: hard ceiling on turns per conversation (also caps cost).

termination.description: INTERACTIVE paradigm only: stopping rule for a conversation.

signal.description: For agent_signal: a stop token an agent may emit to end the conversation.

tools.description: INTERACTIVE paradigm only: allowed tool names. Under LOCAL_MODE the runner permits local-only tools and disables any with external egress.

n_conversations.description: INTERACTIVE paradigm only: number of independent conversations to run. Small-N by design; the runner enforces an upper cap (default 50).
